entry.title: Part 2: Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) with GenAI and RAG
entry.content:
Introduction

The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses critical automation challenges in revenue recognition processes. Traditional SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) systems, while robust, often require significant manual intervention for complex contract analysis and revenue timing decisions. By incorporating LLMs with RAG's precise information retrieval capabilities, organizations can now automate previously manual tasks while significantly reducing errors. The system achieves this by combining the pattern recognition capabilities of LLMs with RAG's ability to ground responses in verified revenue recognition rules and standards. This fusion of technologies provides particular value in complex scenarios such as multi-element arrangements, variable consideration contracts, and timing-sensitive revenue recognition, where traditional rule-based automation often falls short.

 

Enhanced Architecture Overview

The SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting architecture can be enhanced with a sophisticated GenAI/RAG processing layer that seamlessly integrates with existing operational systems and core RAR components. This new layer sits between the operational systems (Sales and Distribution, Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable, and non-SAP systems) and the SAP S/4HANA Revenue Accounting and Reporting engine. Through standardized IC (Integration Component) interfaces, operational data flows into the GenAI/RAG layer where documents undergo intelligent processing via the document grounding component. The system leverages SAP HANA's vector store capabilities for efficient embedding storage and retrieval, while the RAG retrieval engine works in conjunction with the LLM integration layer to enhance the traditional RAR processes. This enhanced architecture maintains the core RAR functionality - including RAI processing, contract management, and BRFplus business rules - while augmenting each step of the five-step revenue recognition model with AI capabilities. The result is a more intelligent system that can handle complex revenue arrangements through automated contract analysis, performance obligation detection, and revenue recognition timing, all while maintaining compliance with IFRS 15 and ASC 606 standards.

 

Integrated System Components

The RAG architecture comprises four essential subsystems working in concert. The document grounding layer handles preprocessing through meta-tagging and intelligent chunking before generating embeddings for vector storage in SAP HANA. A specialized retrieval pipeline manages vector search operations and implements the re-ranking logic for contextual relevance. The feedback subsystem employs machine learning algorithms for continuous weight adjustment based on user interactions, while the generative layer leverages LLMs to synthesize contextually appropriate responses from retrieved information. This modular architecture ensures both system flexibility and maintainable complexity while supporting the specific demands of revenue recognition workflows.

 

Advanced Retrieval Mechanisms

The retrieval architecture implements two sophisticated mechanisms for optimal document processing. The first mechanism (1-4 in fig below) employs a multi-stage search process starting with global vector similarity calculations across all document chunks, followed by local context refinement focused on top-ranked results. A weighted query refinement phase then incorporates high-ranking chunk terms to create context-aware queries, culminating in a final retrieval pass that aggregates the most relevant information.

 

The second mechanism (5 in fig above) utilizes feedback-driven weight adjustment, where initial embeddings and similarity scores form the foundation for an adaptive ML model. This model continuously evolves through user feedback integration, employing scaling factors to adjust chunk weights dynamically. The system stores these refined weights persistently, enabling progressive improvement in retrieval accuracy across multiple queries while maintaining complete audit trails for revenue recognition compliance.

Vector Database Integration

SAP HANA's vector database capabilities form the backbone of the retrieval system, enabling efficient storage and querying of high-dimensional embeddings. The database architecture supports real-time indexing of new documents while maintaining rapid query performance through optimized index structures. Automated synchronization ensures consistency between the vector store and traditional relational data.

Performance Optimization

Advanced caching mechanisms and query optimization techniques ensure rapid response times even with large document volumes. The system implements intelligent batching for vector operations, maximizing GPU utilization during embedding generation. Feedback-driven weight adjustments continuously refine the model's performance, with cosine similarity rates improving from 88.15% to over 95% through iterative optimization.

Future Outlook

As retrieval technologies evolve, the integration with revenue management systems will become increasingly sophisticated. Emerging developments in sparse attention mechanisms and efficient indexing strategies promise to further enhance retrieval accuracy while reducing computational overhead. These advancements will enable more nuanced understanding of complex revenue arrangements while maintaining strict compliance with evolving standards.

read part 1 here: Part 1: SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR)... - SAP Community

 

 #SAP #RevenueRecognition #SAPRAR #AIinFinance #GenAI #RAG #LargeLanguageModels #MachineLearning #SAPBTP #ComplianceAutomation #SAPHANA #RevenueManagement #AdvancedRetrieval #FinancialInnovation #DigitalTransformation #ContractAnalysis #MultiElementArrangements #BusinessIntelligence #IFRS15 #ASC606 #VectorDatabase #AutomationInnovation

entry.title: SAP BTP Hackathon at SAPHILA 2025 (AFSUG Members)
entry.content:
This year we launched the first-ever AFSUG and SAP collaboration SAP BTP Hackathon.

In a nutshell:

  • All AFSUG members can nominate a business use case that showcases SAP BTP capabilities
  • Up to 25 customers will be selected to build out their use case and compete for a chance to demo it live on stage at SAPHILA 2025
  • 8 finalists will participate in the first-ever SAPHILA Demo Jam in June for a chance to win some cool prizes

When talking to Amanda Gibbs, the CEO of the African SAP User Group (AFSUG), she shared her thoughts:

"The AFSUG SAP BTP Hackathon is an extraordinary opportunity for our members to showcase their creativity, problem-solving skills, and innovative thinking. By collaborating with SAP on the SAPHILA Hackathon, we’re creating a platform where customers and partners can explore the transformative potential of SAP BTP, turning bold ideas into tangible solutions that deliver real business value. At AFSUG, we believe in the power of community and shared learning. This hackathon is not just about competition; it’s about fostering connections, gaining insights, and driving meaningful change together. I encourage all our members to seize this chance to innovate, learn from SAP’s global experts, and be part of something truly impactful."

I really can’t beat that intro, so I am not even going to try 🙂

 

The Details

SAP BTP is THE platform that brings together application development, automation, data management, analytics and planning, integration, and AI capabilities into one unified environment optimized for integrating and extending SAP applications.

It’s used by customers, partners and SAP to innovate and optimize business processes while leveraging the latest and greatest cloud technology.

The SAP BTP hackathon challenge will showcase how customers can use SAP BTP to adapt and optimize their business processes. But that’s not all!

AFSUG members will also benefit from in-depth enablement sessions led by SAP product experts to help them through the ideation and build phases.

The participating customers, those who have had their use case selected for the SAP BTP hackathon, will also have access to expert coaches during their build phase. Coaches are there to make sure “no use case is left behind” 😉

Once the build phase is completed, customers will pitch their use case to a jury of SAP experts. The jury will choose 8 finalists based on different criteria, like business relevance, SAP BTP usage, innovation and vision, that will go on to demo their use case in front of a live audience at SAPHILA 2025.

To show off your efforts, participating customers will get special badges added to their SAP Community profile. So, if you are not yet an SAP Community member, what are you waiting for?

 

To All AFSUG Members

Don’t miss this unique opportunity to participate in the SAP BTP hackathon!

In short:

  1. Submit your use case here by February 18
  2. (Selected customers) Build your prototype by April 22
  3. Get ready to pitch your demo from May 2 to May 16
  4. (Finalists) Compete in the Demo Jam at SAPHILA 2025 on June 3

In case you still need convincing, let me reiterate some of the perks:

  • Attend SAP BTP enablement sessions led by SAP experts for inspiration and knowledge.
  • Engage with the SAP ecosystem and get special badges to display on your profile. Don’t forget to create an account in the SAP Community
  • Get direct access to SAP BTP coaches to support you during your build phase.
  • Expand your SAP BTP knowledge and bridge the gap between business and IT.

 

Conclusion

We are beyond excited to see what our customers come up and see their ideas come to life with SAP Business Technology Platform.

And yes, we would love to bring this format to other user groups and locations. One step at a time 😉

entry.title: Implico at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference 2025
entry.content:
As the energy sector navigates rapid digital transformation and sustainability challenges, Implico is at the forefront of delivering innovative solutions to optimize supply chains and enhance fuel retail operations. From April 8-10, 2025, in Rotterdam, we will join leading energy and technology experts at the SAP for Energy and Utilities Conference and showcase how cutting-edge automation and SAP solutions can empower companies to adapt and thrive in a changing market. 

We are proud to present two key sessions at this event: a workshop session on the Pre-Conference Day April 8 and a co-customer presentation at the main event on April 9, both featuring real-world applications of our solutions.

 

1. Optimizing Fuel Retail Operations with SAP Solutions 

Speakers: Jean-Marc Delbos (SAP) & Thomas Weggelaar (Implico) 

Efficient management of fuel retail networks is essential for profitability and customer satisfaction. This joint session with Jean-Marc Delbos from SAP and Thomas Weggelaar from Implico will focus on enhancing the end-to-end processes of fuel retail operations using SAP’s Retail Fuel Network Operations (SAP S/4HANA RFNO) solution and Implico’s automation technologies. 

 Key Takeaways: 

  • Unlocking operational efficiencies through SAP S/4HANA RFNO and automated data flows. 
  • Real-time fuel inventory monitoring and replenishment. 
  • Reducing operational overhead and carbon emissions through process automation. 

 

2. Automatic Trip Planning for Bulk Logistics 

Speaker: Adrian Mägerle (Migrol/Switzerland) & André Bente (Implico) 

(IDM) by Implico is a robust solution designed to enhance capabilities within SAP S4SCSD. Fuel distribution logistics is a complex puzzle that requires precision and efficiency. In this session, Adrian Mägerle from Migrol AG, one of Switzerland’s leading energy services providers, will explore together with André Bente from Implico how automatic trip planning optimizes the logistics in supply chain and secondary distribution by leveraging SAP-integrated tools and Implico’s Integrated Dispatch Management (IDM) capabilities. 

Key Takeaways: 

  • Reducing manual planning efforts  
  • Boosting efficiency and sustainability 
  • Insights from a prototype of the new Implico SAPlement AddTCO 

 

Enhancing Sustainability through Digitalization and the Clean Core Approach 

As an SAP SOLEX Partner, Implico’s approach to sustainability focuses on combining digitalization, automation, and optimized resource allocation with SAP’s Clean Core approach, which ensures that custom business processes are decoupled from the digital core of SAP systems. This strategy allows for easier upgrades, continuous innovation, and long-term system stability. 

By leveraging clean, structured data and the Clean Core methodology, Implico enables: 

  • Efficient transport routes that reduce fuel consumption and emissions. 
  • Real-time energy usage monitoring and sustainability tracking. 
  • Smarter asset utilization to minimize waste and maximize operational efficiency. 

These solutions are designed to meet both current needs and the challenges of a greener future, including the integration of LNG, hydrogen, and other renewable fuels into existing networks. 

 

Join Us in Rotterdam for Insightful Discussions and Live Demos 

We invite attendees to visit our booth for hands-on demos of our solutions and to attend our presentations to learn how we’re transforming supply chains in the energy sector. Whether you’re planning an S/4HANA migration or seeking ways to optimize fuel retail operations, Implico is here to help. 

 

Our Product Portfolio: Driving Efficiency Across the Supply Chain 

Implico’s comprehensive portfolio empowers businesses to streamline processes, reduce costs, and improve customer satisfaction.  

Learn more about our core offerings: 

  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** (Secondary Distribution Management) - Optimizing last-mile fuel delivery through route planning, dispatching, and real-time monitoring. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** (Retail Fuel Network Operations) - Enhancing visibility into fuel station inventory, pricing, and replenishment needs. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** - Automating route planning and dispatch to reduce downtime and maximize resource efficiency. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION ***- Simplifying and automating processes in bulk liquid handling, from loading to transport. 
  • *** REMOVED BY MODERATION ***- Streamlining cylinder management, automating order processing, and optimizing distribution for maximum efficiency. 

 

Let’s shape the future of energy together! 

Schedule a meeting with our team:  

Visit *** REMOVED BY MODERATION *** to contact us or book a session during the event. 

 

We look forward to seeing you in Rotterdam from April 8-10, 2025!

entry.title: The power of AI Agents: My Journey with LangGraph
entry.content:
AI agents are transforming the landscape of artificial intelligence, enabling a new level of autonomous decision-making, intelligent task execution and automation. This blog dives into the implementation of an AI-powered chat assistant, built with LangGraph, that transforms sales workflows by allowing sales agents to search for customers, manage transactions, and generate customer insights using natural language

First, what are AI Agents?

AI Agents are software programs that uses AI techniques like ML or LLM to make decisions and perform actions based on the information it has. LLMs can understand complex user requests expressed in natural language which allows the agent program to interpret what users truly want. LLMs can determine the control flow of an application and automate a wide range of tasks.

In general, LLMs provide the "brains" and "voice" that enable AI agents to understand and respond to users in a natural way and plan and execute complex tasks

Types of Agents and its challenges:

AI Agents can be categorized based on their control flow and the level of autonomy given to the LLM,

  • Router-based agents have a narrow control flow and the LLM gets to control a single step in a flow
  • Fully autonomous agents can have any sequence of steps and the LLM gets to choose the steps based on the given resources

The amount of control given to the LLM is very low in a router-based agent and very high in a fully autonomous agent.

Challenge: As the LLM-powered Agent’s level of control increases, the reliability of the application decreases that is, as the LLM is given more control, it operates with less guardrails. Fully autonomous agents have fewer predefined rules or constraints which means there's less oversight over the LLM's decision-making process. Therefore, there is a tradeoff between autonomy and reliability.

LangGraph:

LangGraph is a framework for building agentic applications. It strikes a balance between LLM control and application reliability by enabling structured workflows while allowing AI agents to make intelligent decisions. LangGraph introduces a graph-based execution model, where different components can operate independently or in parallel.

At the core of LangGraph are nodes, edges, and state schema, which define how data flows and decisions are made:

  • Nodes → Represent individual steps in an AI agent’s workflow. Each node can perform an action, such as calling an LLM, retrieving data, or executing a tool.
  • Edges → Edges define the connection between nodes. Edges can be static or dynamic, that is, we can instruct the graph to choose a node based on a certain condition. These are called conditional edges.
  • State Schema → Stores the message throughout the workflow, entire graph context(information) is stored and passed between nodes

By leveraging parallel execution, persistent memory, and human-in-the-loop capabilities, LangGraph enables scalable, intelligent AI agents that integrate seamlessly into real-world applications.

AI Agent chat assistant - Use Case

The AI-powered agentic chat assistant is connected to an SAP Sales Cloud tenant. The chat assistant is designed to help sales agents seamlessly search for customers and manage transaction data using natural language interactions. Powered by LLM, the assistant understands user intent and dynamically determines the appropriate tool function to execute enabling agents to retrieve customer details, create or update quotations and sales orders, and generate a 360-degree customer profile, which consolidates activities, leads, quotations, sales orders, and service tickets.

This chatbot implementation utilizes LangGraph’s advanced capabilities, including parallelization, sub-graphs, persistence, and human-in-the-loop interactions, providing a structured and flexible AI-driven workflow. I’ll dive deeper into these features as we proceed.

Process Flow of AI Agentic Chat Assistant:

  1. The process starts with the user providing input (a prompt) to the system. The user's prompt is processed by the AI Assistant powered by LLM, I have used gpt-35-turbo hosted in SAP GenAI hub.
  2. The LLM understands the user's intent. Based on the user's prompt, the LLM determines if any external tools(functions) are needed and generates "Tool Calls."
  3. For example, if the user prompts “search for customer ArunKumar”, the LLM determines the user need to search for a customer and raises a tool call to “Customer Search” tool function. Similarly, based on user prompt, the LLM raises a tool to update tools or summary tool
  4. If the user wants to create or modify quotations or sales orders, the "Update Tools" functions are used. Since this action will update SAP Sales Cloud, we introduce Human-in-the-loop functionality. Thus, before the data is sent to SAP Sales Cloud the agent gets a user confirmation. Based on the answer provided by the user, the agent will proceed with the action or rollback to the previous state
  5. If the user wants to extract a 360-degree summary of the customer profile, the “Summary Tools” functions are used. The summary tool calls a Sub-graph which in turn makes parallel calls to SAP Sales Cloud to get customer relevant transactional data.
  6. Finally, all the extracted data is sent to AI agent for summarization before sending the information back to the user.

AI Chat Assistant Graph:

State-Schema:

The state schema is the foundation of a graph definition. It serves as the input schema for all nodes and edges within the graph. It defines how data flows throughout the execution.

In our use case, the state schema is implemented using TypedDict, storing user prompts, AI responses, tool messages, and customer and transaction-related information. As the conversation progresses, each new message is dynamically appended to the state.

The schema consists of lists and dictionaries to manage messages, customer details, and transaction data. The "messages" key specifically stores human, AI, and tool messages. To ensure new messages are added seamlessly, it leverages the reducer function add_messages, which appends each incoming message to the existing list.

We basically deal with 3 message types,

HumanMessage stores user prompts
AIMessage stores AI response
ToolMessagestores output returned by tool function execution example, customer search, quotation search

Here’s an example of how the "messages" field in the state schema stores information during an interaction between the user and the AI agent. This also demonstrates how tool calls are handled and integrated into the conversation flow.

User prompt: Hi

{ 'messages': [ HumanMessage( content='Hi' ) ] }

AI Message:  Hello! How can I assist you today?

{ 'messages': [ HumanMessage( content='Hi' ),
AIMessage( content='Hello! How can I assist you today?' ) ] }

User prompt: search for customer porter llc

{
'messages': [
HumanMessage( content='Hi', ),
AIMessage( content='Hello! How can I assist you today?', ) ,
HumanMessage( content='search for customer porter llc', ) ] }

AI Message: Tool Calls: search_customers (call_JeRHjJZHwYTBbSNx76mcgO0a), Args:query: porter llc

{
'messages': [
HumanMessage( content='Hi', ),
AIMessage( content='Hello! How can I assist you today?', ),
HumanMessage( content='search for customer porter llc', ),
AIMessage(
tool_calls=[ {
'name': 'search_customers',
'args': {'query': 'porter llc'},
'type': 'tool_call' } ] ) ] }

Tool Message: {"customer": [{"ObjectID": "00163E038C2E1EE299C1BB0BE9398F9B", "AccountID": "1001", "Name": "Porter LLC"}]}

{
'messages': [
HumanMessage( content='Hi' ),
AIMessage( content='Hello! How can I assist you today?' ),
HumanMessage( content='search for customer porter llc' ),
AIMessage( tool_calls=[ {
'name': 'search_customers',
'args': {'query': 'porter llc'},
'type': 'tool_call' } ] ),
ToolMessage( content='{"customer": [{"ObjectID": "00163E038C2", "AccountID": "1001", "Name": "Porter LLC"}]}',
name='search_customers' ) ] }

AI Message:  I found a customer matching the name "Porter LLC" with the Account ID "1001". Is this the customer you were looking for?

{
'messages': [
HumanMessage( content='Hi' ),
AIMessage( content='Hello! How can I assist you today?' ),
HumanMessage( content='search for customer porter llc' ),
AIMessage( tool_calls=[ {
'name': 'search_customers',
'args': {'query': 'porter llc'},
'type': 'tool_call' } ] ),
ToolMessage( content='{"customer": [{"ObjectID": "00163E038C2", "AccountID": "1001", "Name": "Porter LLC"}]}',
name='search_customers' ),
AIMessage( content='I found a customer matching the name "Porter LLC" with the Account ID "1001". Is this the customer you were looking for?' ) ] }

Persistence:

It’s crucial to manage memory efficiently so the AI agent can retain context throughout the interaction. LangGraph provides a built-in persistence layer to save the state of AI agent's execution. There are 2 components, threads and checkpointers,

  • Threads – represents a single conversationsl session with an AI agent. A thread is defined using a thread ID, each thread has its own state, keeping it independent from other sessions. Memory is scoped only to that thread, meaning a different user session won't have access to another user’s conversation history.
  • Checkpointers – Its also possible to persist the state of a thread in a database. This is used to resume the conversation even after the application is restarted. This is like how ChatGPT stores a conversation and allows user to resume it at any point. LangGraph provides built-in checkpointing (state persistence) with SQLite through the SqliteCheckpoint module.

In our use case, we generate a thread_id for each interaction and pass it at the time of execution. This thread-based state management, makes it possible to resume conversations for a specific user. The checkpointer MemorySaver() ensures that the state is stored and retrieved based on the thread_id.

memory = MemorySaver()
graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=memory, interrupt_before=["update_tools"])

config = {
"configurable": {
"thread_id": thread_id,
}
}
events = graph.stream(
{"messages": ("user", user_input)}, config, stream_mode="values"
)

Tools:

In LangGraph, each node in the graph represents a function that processes input and returns an updated state. When defining a graph, functions are added as nodes and connected using edges. The state schema flows between nodes, allowing functions to process and modify it.

In LangGraph its possible to register a tool function with @tool annotation and let the LLM decide when to invoke these tools. These functions bound to the LLM and are dynamically called based on the user prompt.

In our use case, we have tool functions grouped in lists “search tools”, “update tools” and “summary tools”. The reason for grouping these tool functions in separate list is to have more control and flexible over the workflow.

Building the graph by defining nodes and connecting edges

Graph generated from above code,

Conditional routing:

In LangGraph, conditional edges allow dynamic routing between nodes based on the state. In our use case, The "Assistant" node is connected to "Search_Tools," "Update_Tools," and "Summary_Tools". However, it doesn’t execute all three in parallel. Instead, it uses a conditional edge controlled by the route_tools function to determine the next step.

def route_tools(state: State):
"""Route execution based on the last tool call."""
# print("route_tools function triggered!")
next_node = tools_condition(state)
# If no search_tools are invoked, return to the user
if next_node == END:
return END
ai_message = state["messages"][-1]
if hasattr(ai_message, "tool_calls") and ai_message.tool_calls:
first_tool_call = ai_message.tool_calls[0]
if first_tool_call["name"] in update_tool_names:
return "update_tools"
elif first_tool_call["name"] == "extract_unified_profile":
return "summary_tools"
return "search_tools"
return "update_state"

This function inspects the last tool call and directs execution accordingly:

  • If an update tool was invoked → route to "update_tools".
  • If profile extraction was requested → route to "summary_tools".
  • Otherwise, default to "search_tools".

Human-in-the-loop:

LangGraph allows human intervention at specific points in the workflow instead of the AI making all decisions autonomously. This enables manual review, user approvals before executing critical actions.

Interruptions are provided using interrupt_before and interrupt_after

  •   interrupt_before=["node_name"] → Pauses execution before the specified node runs.
  •   interrupt_after=["node_name"] → Pauses execution after the specified node completes

In our use case, when user tries to create/update quotation or sales order, the AI model will get a user confirmation before sending a HTTP request to SAP Sales Cloud. This is implemented by assigning interrupt_before functionality to  “update_tools” node while compiling the graph.

graph = builder.compile(checkpointer=memory,interrupt_before=["update_tools"],)

The graph execution runs in a loop until the user chooses to exit by typing "quit", "exit", or "q". The workflow pauses whenever the AI model detects a tool call related to update functions like "create_quotation". At this point, the system requests user approval before proceeding. If approved, the tool function executes; otherwise, the operation is canceled.

Sub-graphs and parallelization:

Sub-Graphs are used to encapsulate a sequence of nodes and edges into a graph unit, which can be reused and invoked from other parts of the main graph. Sub-Graphs promote modularity and makes the code cleaner and easier to manage.

In our use case, we have a sub-graph to extract customer transactional data from SAP Sales Cloud. The sub-graph has its own state schema, it receives customer information when called from the main graph. This customer information is used to query transaction data from SAP Sales Cloud.

Here, using LangGraph’s parallelization technique multiple nodes are executed simultaneously. Instead of processing the nodes sequentially, parallel execution enables the graph to trigger all nodes at once. Each node executes independently and the results are merged back into the state. This greatly reduces processing time by running independent tasks simultanouesly. Here is the sub-graph,

When the user requests to extract a 360-degree customer profile, the AI model invokes "Summary Tools", which then triggers the sub-graph. Once the sub-graph execution completes, the state returns to the main graph function "Customer Summary" which generates an internal HumanMessage prompting the AI model to summarize the extracted data from SAP Sales Cloud. The AI model then processes the data and presents a concise summary to the user.

entry.title: SAP Developer News, March 6th, 2025
entry.content:
Podcast Version: https://podcast.opensap.info/sap-developers/2025/03/06/sap-developer-news-march-6th-2025/

DESCRIPTION

AI / ML Roadshow – India

  • SAP CodeJam Community Group:  https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-codejam/gh-p/code-jam

 SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House - AI

  • Blog post https://community.sap.com/t5/artificial-intelligence-and-machine-learning-blogs/sap-developer-challenge-march-sap-community-escape-house-ai/ba-p/14030669

 Code Connect – Call For Proposals closing on 10 March

  • Code Connect website https://code-connect.dev/

Flat Josh travels from Heidelberg to Philly and to NSQ SAP office

Functional Programming episodes on Hands-on SAP Dev?

  • Hands-on SAP Dev show episodes https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQABOpzhd7WI-hMpy99PxUo0
  • The Art and Science of CAP with Daniel Hutzel series https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PL6RpkC85SLQAe45xlhIfhTYB9G0mdRVjI

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 AI / ML Roadshow - India

1:28 SAP Developer Challenge March - SAP Community Escape House – AI

2:08 Functional Programming episodes on Hands-on SAP Dev?

3:03 Flat Josh travels from Heidelberg to Philly and to NSQ SAP office

5:04 Code Connect – Call For Proposals closing on 10 March

Transcription

[Witalij, Nora and Sheena] Namaste! Greetings from Kolkata, where we had first two SAP CodeJams of our ML/AI tour. Nora, what did we do today? We have the Generative AI CodeJam today, where we use the Python SDK of Generative AI Hub, and we also used the orchestration service of Generative AI Hub. we also implemented a retrieval-augmented generation use case, and we even looked into AI agents. What did we do yesterday? Yesterday we had more fun. More fun than today? I'm kidding. Just wanted to check that we are still alive at the end of these two days, and luckily we are. So yesterday we focused on HANA embedded machine learning, and basically we were going through several cycles of training the machine learning model with the best parameters. And I think that the feedback was great both yesterday and today, but this is not the end of our show. So, Sheena, where are we going next? Yeah, we are heading out to Pune to conduct another AI CodeJam at Bloomberg. And we are not stopping there. We are coming back to Bangalore and conducting two other CodeJams on ML and AI at TCS. So see you soon. See you. Bye.

[Shilpa] Hi, Nora and Witalij. Welcome to India and I'm glad you're having some great CodeJams here. While you're on the way to the next CodeJam, I want to talk about the March developer challenge on your behalf. So with that, hello SAP developers. A new escape room in the SAP community escape house has been unlocked and it's the AI room. If you want to try your hands on the Generative AI hub on SAP AI core, learn how to use orchestration service and SAP HANA vector engine, then do check out the challenge. The room will be unlocked until the end of March. Check it out, have fun and escape the room.

[DJ] The developer advocates run a live stream show called Hands on SAP Dev, as I'm sure some, hopefully many of you already know. Recently, we've been running a series called the Art and Science of Cap with Daniel Hutzel, where we've discussed and talked about and dug into in great depth lots of different influences that have shaped CAP its design. from an art and science perspective. One of these influences is functional programming. And there was chat in the most recent episode, that's part 10, as to whether we should also do some functional programming episodes in general, not specific to CAP. Some folks in the chat during the live stream, let me know that they would like to see some of these episodes. If you want to see some sessions on functional programming from us, then let us know in the comments. Thanks.

[Josh] It's a long way from Philly to Heidelberg. It's also a long way back from Heidelberg to Philly. Luckily, we've got our best. Well, we've got this guy on it. Oh, sorry, Josh.

[DJ] Code Connect, the annual conference for SAP technical practitioners around the world is taking place again in July. First of all, we have a warm-up in the form of a CodeJam, two CodeJams in fact. Then we have UI5Con, followed by reCAP, followed by HANA TechCon. Now, for these three conferences, UI5Con, reCAP and HANA TechCon, there's currently a call for proposals, talks, talk submissions. But the call for proposals is ending shortly, March the 10th. So get your submissions in to one, two or all three of these one-day conferences. The possible session types are many unvaried, short talks, long talks, demos, workshops, hands-on sessions, all sorts of different possibilities. So check out the CodeConnect website, wave and jump to each of the individual. conferences and find the call for proposals submission forms. Good look and I'll see you there.

entry.title: Experience at SAP CodeJam Kolkata 2025 || 4th March & 5th March, 2025
entry.content:
Reflecting on SAP CodeJam 2025 - Kolkata 


I’m thrilled to share my experience attending the SAP CodeJam 2025 in Kolkata, driven by SAP Developer advocates @noravonthenen , @Vitaliy-R , @sheenamk  and @eshrinivasan  hosted by Capgemini this year! The session centered around Machine Learning (ML) with Python and Generative AI (GenAI) in SAP, and it was an eye-opener into how these groundbreaking technologies are shaping the future of enterprise solutions.

Some key insights that stood out:

🤖 Empowering SAP Consultants with Real-Time AI: We discussed how GenAI and ML can help SAP consultants tackle real-time challenges. AI is enhancing efficiency and reducing the time spent on manual interventions. This is a game-changer in real-time project scenarios!

📊 Predictive Analytics for Smarter Decisions: With ML models embedded in SAP for smarter decision-making, such as optimizing processes, forecasting demand, and identifying risks—leading to more proactive, data-driven strategies.

⚡ Real-Time Problem Solving: GenAI tools are revolutionizing issue resolution during SAP implementations. By offering automated, instant troubleshooting, consultants can focus on high-value strategic decisions rather than spending time on repetitive system fixes.

🔧 Tailored Solutions at Speed: The ability of GenAI to generate custom code, configurations, and solutions in real-time allows SAP consultants to swiftly address client needs, significantly reducing implementation times while delivering highly personalized, impactful outcomes.

Here are some use cases and benefits of GenAI and ML with Python in SAP:

Use Cases:

  1. Predictive Analytics:

    • Use Case: Use ML models to forecast demand, optimize inventory, and predict financial trends in SAP.
    • Benefit: Helps businesses make data-driven decisions, reduce costs, and improve forecasting accuracy.

  2. Process Automation (RPA):

    • Use Case: Automate repetitive tasks like data entry, report generation, or invoice processing using Python scripts and GenAI.
    • Benefit: Reduces manual labor, minimizes errors, and accelerates process completion.

  3. Customer Insights and Personalization:

    • Use Case: Leverage ML to analyze customer behavior data and create personalized product recommendations in SAP.
    • Benefit: Improves customer satisfaction and drives sales by delivering more tailored experiences.

  4. Anomaly Detection:

    • Use Case: Detect outliers or unusual transactions in financial or inventory data using ML models trained in Python.
    • Benefit: Identifies potential fraud or errors quickly, enhancing security and data integrity.

  5. Automated Reporting and Dashboards:

    • Use Case: Use GenAI to generate custom reports and dashboards based on business parameters in SAP.
    • Benefit: Provides real-time insights without manual report creation, improving decision-making speed.

  6. Supply Chain Optimization:

    • Use Case: Use ML algorithms to optimize supply chain processes in SAP, from procurement to delivery.
    • Benefit: Reduces operational costs, minimizes delays, and enhances inventory management.

Benefits:

  1. Enhanced Efficiency:

    • Automates repetitive tasks, reducing manual effort and increasing overall system efficiency.

  2. Improved Accuracy:

    • ML models enhance decision-making accuracy by providing data-driven insights and predictions.

  3. Cost Savings:

    • Reduces costs by improving forecasting, streamlining processes, and identifying inefficiencies.

  4. Scalability:

    • Python and AI-powered models can scale to meet the growing data needs of businesses, ensuring systems can handle increased complexity over time.

  5. Faster Decision-Making:

    • Real-time insights from GenAI and ML lead to quicker and more informed decisions.

By integrating Python, GenAI, and ML into SAP, businesses can unlock automation, predictive capabilities, and smarter analytics, driving significant business value.

Apart from these, many key-points are taken away i.e. types of ML ( Supervised, Unsupervised, Reinforcement Learning ), Training a data model, Business Data Cloud, Data Preparation, Feature Engineering, Data Frame, EDA etc. from @Vitaliy-R on 4th March, Getting Started with Machine Learning using SAP HANA. Again on 5th March, Getting Started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core session by @noravonthenen key-points ( AI Buzzwords )are taken away i.e. Fine Tuning, Orchestration including Grounding, Prompt Templating, Content Filter, Data Masking, LLM Access and all the hands-on procedure i.e. define Orchestration service deployment, AI model deployment etc.

Kudos to Chatrath Harinder Singh, @Sharmistha22 , Goutam Dasgupta, Abhishek Guha Roy for helping to make this event successful.

Also thank you @DataTherapist for believing in me to make this event a success. Also, thank you @Raya_16 and @TanushreeD  to make this event in Capgemini.

@ShilpaSAP33 @lauraschmitz @thomasjungblu @Abhishek_10 @KamallicaGhosh1 @Soham_SCM @SohamBhKoL 

Also sharing some of the glimpses of the SAP CodeJam Kolkata 2025.


#SAP #AI #MachineLearning #GenAI #FutureOfSAP #Capgemini #SAPCodeJam2025 #SAPRoadshow

entry.title: 【日本語化】Generative AI with SAP - Part5 - SAP AI Coreの概要
entry.content:
このブログはEcosystem Growth活動の一環で「Generative AI with SAP（@Raja_Gupta1）」シリーズを日本語化・加筆したものです。このシリーズでは、SAPがポートフォリオ全体でどのように AI および Generative AI を活用しているかを理解することに焦点を当てています。

このシリーズで学べること：

  • SAPがポートフォリオ全体で AI および Generative AI をどのように活用しているか
  • SAP Business AI とは何か、そしてSAPのパートナーや顧客がそれをどのように活用できるのか
  • SAP AI Core・SAP AI Launchpad・Joule とは何か
  • SAPのAI戦略においてSAP BTPが果たす役割
  • 実際のユースケースやハンズオン事例など

理解しやすくするため、このブログシリーズは短いセクションに分けられています。それぞれのブログは、10～15分程度で学習できる内容です。

  • Part1 - SAP Business AIの概要
  • Part2 - SAP Business AIの構成要素
  • Part3 - Joule：SAPのGenerative AI Copilot
  • Part4 - SAP BTPにおけるAI Foundation 
  • Part5 - SAP AI Coreの概要 [現在のブログ]
  • Part6 - SAP AI Launchpadの概要
  • Part7 - SAP AI CoreとSAP AI Launchpadの使い方
  • Part8 - SAP AI CoreにおけるHello World
  • Part9 - Generative AI Hubの概要
  • Part10 - SAP HANA Cloud vector engine
  • Part11 - RAG（Retrieval-Augmented Generation）・SAP AI Core・Generative AI Hub・HANA Cloud vector engine を用いた実際のユースケース

このブログはシリーズの第5回目であり、SAP AI Coreについて学びます。

Note: 後続のブログも順次公開予定です。

SAP AI Coreをより深く理解するために、SAP AI Coreに関するいくつかの重要な用語を確認しましょう。

 

AIモデルとは何か？

AIモデルとは、データからパターンを学習して特定のタスクを実行するように訓練されたコンピュータプログラムを指します。一般にAIモデルには、膨大なサイズのデータを処理しデータ内部の潜在的なパターンを抽出するための複雑な計算手法で構成されています。

以下の図は、AIモデルに関する重要なポイントをまとめたものです。

AIモデルを学習させるには、膨大なデータセットが必要です。学習データには、テキスト・画像・ビデオ・数値などの様々な形式のデータを含みます。強力なAIモデルには、インターネット上に公開されているデータを使用して学習されているものもあります。

Note: AIモデルについて詳しく知りたい方は「What is AI Model?」を参考にしてください。

 

AIランタイムとは何か？

AIランタイムとは、AIモデル（またはアルゴリズム）が実行される環境またはプラットフォームを指します。

AIランタイムには、CPUやGPU等のハードウェア・TensorFlowやPyTorch等のソフトウェアフレームワーク・推論サーバ等のサポートサービスといった、AIモデルを本番環境で実行するために必要なツールやリソースが含まれます。また、AIランタイムは、AIモデルを効率的にデプロイ・管理・実行するために使用されます。

AIランタイムの例は以下の通りです：

  • TensorFlow Serving
  • Amazon SageMaker
  • Google AI Platform
  • Azure Machine Learning
  • SAP AI Core

 

AIワークフローとは何か？

一般に、ワークフローとは、ある作業を始めてから終わるまでの一連の手順やプロセスのことを指します。例えば、生のデータから、加工・処理された状態なるまでの道筋を指します。

AIワークフローは、AIモデルを使って手作業が多く繰り返されるようなビジネスプロセスを効率化・改善するためのワークフローです。

 

推論サーバーとは何か？

AIモデルの学習が完了した際、リアルタイムでの予測やテキスト生成などを実現するために運用環境へのデプロイが必要です。このような処理を担うのが推論サーバーです。

推論サーバーは学習済みのモデルをホストし、ユーザーまたはアプリケーションからの要求を処理して、新しい入力データに基づいて予測や生成内容を出力します。

言い換えれば、推論サーバーは、特にリアルタイムでの予測や推論を行うために、機械学習モデルをデプロイして実行するようにデザインされた専門的なサーバーであると言えます。

 

推論サーバーの具体例

NVIDIA Tritonと呼ばれる推論サーバーは、AIモデルを大規模に展開するためのスケーラブルで柔軟な推論サーバーです。Tritonは、GPT-2やGPT-3などのモデルをホストして提供することができます。

 

それでは、メインのトピックである SAP AI Core について考えましょう！

 

SAP AI Coreとは何か？

SAP AI Coreは、SAP BTP上で利用可能な「強力なAIランタイムを提供する」サービスです。SAP AI Coreを使用して、AIワークフローとAIモデルを実行することができます。

SAP AI Coreは、AI 資産（AI モデルやコンポーネントなど）を、特定のクラウドベンダーに依存せず、拡張性と標準化を保ちながら実行・運用できるように設計されたプラットフォームです。

 

SAP AI Coreにおけるいくつかの重要なポイント

  • SAP AI Coreは、SAPが提供する高負荷（ヘビーロード）のAI処理向けランタイムです。これにより、大規模なAIモデルでも効率的かつコストを抑えて学習・デプロイできます
  • SAP AI Coreは、SAP AI Launchpadとネイティブに統合されており、SAP AI Launchpadを通して簡単にAIワークフローの管理やプロセス・タスクの運用を実行できます
  • SAP AI Coreには、PostmanなどのツールやPythonを使ったプログラムによるアクセスにも対応しています
  • SAP AI Coreは、AIシナリオのライフサイクル全体をサポートするため、開発から運用まで一貫して管理できます

 

SAP AI Coreの機能と特徴

SAP AI Coreが提供する主な機能は、モデルの学習やバッチ推論といったAIワークフローを「オーケストレーション（複数のタスクやプロセスを統合・管理し、全体を最適に制御すること）」し、さらに推論機能を提供することです。

以下の図には、SAP AI Coreの様々な機能と特徴がまとめられています

それでは、上の図をわかりやすく説明しましょう！

標準化されたインターフェース

SAP AI Core上で動作するAIモデルは、標準化されたインターフェースを利用することで、さまざまなビジネスアプリケーションと簡単に連携できます。この標準インターフェース（「AI API」と呼ばれます）により、AIモデルの統合に加えて、SAP AI Launchpad（またはPostmanやPythonなど）を通じたSAP AI Coreの管理・監視・運用が可能になります。

AI APIとは何か？

AI APIとは、SAP AI Coreが提供する標準化されたAIインターフェースです。これにより、ビジネスアプリケーションとの接続や、SAP AI Launchpad 上でのAIシナリオ（予測・推論など）運用を実現するために利用できます。

AI API自体は独立した製品ではなく、AIシナリオの利用や運用管理を行うための共通フレームワークを定義したものです。この仕様により、ビジネスアプリケーションからの一貫した利用などを実現することができます。言い換えると、AI APIは、SAP AI Coreが管理するAI資産（学習済みモデルなど）やシナリオ、ワークフローをまとめて扱うための抽象化レイヤーでもあります。これにより、複雑な実装を意識せずに、標準化された手順で管理や連携を行えます。

Note: AI APIの詳細についてはREST API | SAP AI CoreやAI API Referenceを参照してください。

 

フルライフサイクル管理

SAP AI Coreは、GitOps（インフラとアプリケーションの両方を含めたシステム全体のコードをGitによて管理する手法）の考え方を取り入れたコンテンツのデプロイなど、AIシナリオのライフサイクル全体を管理する機能を提供します。
この仕組みは、Argo Workflows（Kubernetes上でワークフローを管理・自動化するためのオープンソースツール）やKFServing（機械学習モデルのデプロイや推論を容易にするオープンソースソリューション）などの最先端のオープンソース技術を基盤として構築されています。

 

インフラを自由に選択

SAP AI Coreの大きな特徴の1つは、ストレージやCPU・GPUを含む多様なサービスプランの中から、柔軟にインフラを選択できることです。

需要に応じて、異なるタスクごとに異なるインフラリソースを使い分けるようSAP AI Coreを設定することが可能です。そのために、SAP AI Coreではあらかじめ複数の「リソースプラン」が用意されています。

Note: 利用できるリソースプランの一覧についてはChoose a Resource Planを参照してください。

 

SAPが管理するデプロイでAIアプリケーション開発をシンプルに

SAP AI Coreを使えば、AIモデルのデプロイをSAPが管理してくれるため、複雑な管理作業を意識する必要がなくなります。例えば、ビジネスアプリケーションから利用できるようにAPIエンドポイントを公開するだけでAI機能を組み込むことができます。
また、SAP AI Coreは、AIをビジネスアプリケーションに統合するためにシンプルなAPIエンドポイントを提供しているため、短時間で効率よくAI機能を導入できます。

 

コストとパフォーマンスのバランスを最適化

SAP AI Coreを使うと、GPUを活用して高負荷なAIユースケースを大規模に高速処理できる一方で、コストをしっかりコントロールすることも可能です。内蔵されたオートスケーリング機能や「スケール・トゥ・ゼロ（※）」の仕組みに加え、ストレージ・CPU・GPUなど幅広いサービスプランから必要に応じて選択することで、効率よく運用しながら費用対効果を最大化できます。

※ スケール・トゥ・ゼロ（Scale-to-zero）とは、アプリケーションやAIモデルを使用していないときには自動的にリソースをゼロ（全く使わない状態）まで縮退させてコストを削減し、リクエストが来たら必要に応じて再び起動して処理を行い、その後またゼロに戻す仕組みを指します。

 

SAPアプリケーションや業務プロセスにAI機能を手軽に組み込む

SAP AI Coreを使用し、SAPアプリケーションへの標準化された統合を活用して、AIユースケースを迅速に構築し、アプリケーションに組み込むことができます。

 

CI/CD（継続的インテグレーション／継続的デリバリー）とマルチテナント対応

新しいAIシナリオを素早く提供するために、SAP AI CoreにはCI/CD機能が備わっています。また、マルチテナントによってテナントごとに環境を分離できます。さらに、オンデマンドで必要なリソースだけを利用し、GPUの高いパフォーマンスと拡張性を活かすことも可能です。

これらの機能は全てはじめから利用される形で提供され、SAPが管理しているため、ユーザーは余計な手間をかけずに利用することができます。このような様々なAIフレームワークを扱える「オープン性」を維持しており、ユーザーは自社のAIシナリオを簡単にデプロイできます。

 

開発ツール：ライブラリと SDK

SAP AI Coreには開発工程を簡略化するためのツールが用意されており、その1つがSAP AI Core SDKです。

SAP AI Core SDKはPythonベースのSDKで、Pythonのメソッドやデータ構造を通じてSAP AI Coreにアクセスできるようにします。これを使うと、SAP AI Core上で簡単にシナリオやワークフローを管理することができます。

Note: SDK の詳細やインストール方法についてはai-core-sdk · PyPIを参照してください。

 

これまでの説明で、SAP AI Coreとは何か、その主な機能についてイメージをつかめたのではないのでしょうか？さらに詳しく知りたい方は、SAP AI Coreの公式ドキュメントを参照してください。

 

What’s Next?

Part6 - SAP AI Launchpadの概要

 

免責事項
本ブログに記載されているすべての見解および意見は、私個人のものであり、個人的な立場で述べられたものです。SAPは、本ブログに掲載された内容について一切の責任を負いませんので、あらかじめご了承ください。

entry.title: Document Information Extraction – Q1 2025 Highlights
entry.content:
Find out what’s new in the Document Information Extraction service.

 

New Excel, Email and Text File Formats

Document Information Extraction, premium edition now supports new Excel, email, and text file types, such as CSV, XLSX, XLSM, DOCX, EMLX, MSG, PAGES, RTF, and TXT. See the complete list sorted by document types in Supported File Types (Premium Edition).

 

New Invoice Header Fields

You can now extract a more comprehensive list of address-related header fields from invoice documents. The list includes Buyer City, Supplier City, Buyer Country Code, and Supplier Country Code. See all of the standard header fields supported by Document Information Extraction in Extracted Header Fields.

 

Decommissioning and Deprecation

As announced in May 2024, the Enrichment Data API Endpoint: Delete Enrichment Data (Synchronous) (DELETE /data) has been decommissioned and is no longer available. Please use the endpoint Delete Enrichment Data (Asynchronous) (DELETE /data/jobs) to delete data records. For more information, check out Delete Enrichment Data (Synchronous) - Decommissioned | SAP Help Portal and Delete Enrichment Data (Asynchronous) | SAP Help Portal.

The Document API endpoint Get Request Payload (GET /document/jobs/{id}/request) is now deprecated and is scheduled for decommissioning in September 2025. Following that date, the endpoint will no longer be available. Find more details in the blog post: Document Information Extraction – Deprecation of D... - SAP Community.

 

Support for the Identity Authentication Service

As of January 2025, you can subscribe to the Document Information Extraction UI using the Identity Authentication service to handle authentication and authorization tasks in SAP BTP. Find all the details in Subscribing to the Document Information Extraction UI With the Identity Authentication Service.

 

Instant Learning  

If you’ve activated instant learning in Document Information Extraction, premium edition, the service can now use your feedback to select the best model for individual fields in SAP schemas for the standard document types: invoice, payment advice, and purchase order. For more information, see Instant Learning (Premium Edition) and Instant Learning (Premium Edition): Best Practices.

 

Export and Import Feature for Instant Learning

You can now export and import documents that you've edited and confirmed for use with instant learning. This new feature of the Document Information Extraction UI allows you to quickly and easily export documents along with all information relevant for instant learning and import them for use in another context. See Export/Import Documents. 

You can also export documents and associated information, such as ground truth and related instant learning data, via the new Document API endpoint Export Document Details.

 

More Meaningful Extraction Confidence Ranges

Document Information Extraction, premium edition now provides more meaningful extraction confidence ranges for fields with the setup type auto and no default extractor – before you enter any feedback on results. The service does so by checking whether the data type of the extracted information matches the data type for the respective field in the schema.

 

Stay up to date with all the latest developments for Document Information Extraction – by following the tag #DocumentInformationExtraction.

entry.title: 🚀 Discover the Future of Transformation, AI, and Customer Support at SAP Sapphire 2025 in Orlando
entry.content:
From May 19–21, 2025, SAP Sapphire in Orlando promises an exciting lineup of insights, innovations, and real-world stories designed to inspire and empower. Whether you're leading a digital transformation, optimizing support processes, or exploring new technologies or product features, this year’s sessions offer practical strategies and firsthand experience from SAP experts and customers alike.

We’re particularly excited to highlight a curated set of sessions focused on business transformation, AI-powered support, SAP Cloud ALM, clean core journeys, and smooth customer onboarding. You’ll hear from industry leaders, customer voices, and SAP experts on how the integrated toolchain, agentic AI, and cloud innovation are shaping the next generation of enterprise success.

Mark your calendars and plan your visit—these are sessions you won’t want to miss.
